C-REMOVE-RTK-RECOMMENDATIONS.IntentSummary: Remove the harmful RTK (rtk-ai/rtk CLI proxy) recommendation from the interactive vvoc init outro and the README "Optional: RTK" section. RTK proxies developer commands and distorts the shape of their output, which confuses LLM sessions and pushes them to work around RTK instead of doing the intended work; the recommendation is net-negative and is removed outright with no replacement.

Baseline.Note: spec.xml is approved and has exactly one direct C-REMOVE-RTK-RECOMMENDATIONS wrapper.
RTK is referenced in exactly two project-authored places: the README.md "## Optional: RTK" section, and the src/commands/init.ts interactive p.outro() block.

src/commands/init.ts has a MODULE_CONTRACT, MODULE_MAP, and CHANGE_SUMMARY; only the CHANGE_SUMMARY needs a new LAST_CHANGE entry, because removing a log line does not change the module contract or exported symbols.
No test asserts on the RTK outro text; src/commands/init.test.ts does not reference RTK or the outro, so no test removal or rewrite is required.
V-M-CLI-INIT tracks src/commands/init.test.ts and bun run build; neither is affected by removing the RTK log lines.

Target.Note: The interactive init outro is reduced to the single success message "vvoc initialized successfully" with no RTK URL or install command.
README.md no longer has an "Optional: RTK" section, rtk-ai/rtk link, or any claim that init recommends RTK.
A repository-wide search for RTK returns no project-authored references, ignoring transitive dependencies recorded in bun.lock.

ScopeDecisions.Note: CHANGELOG.md is auto-generated by release:bump (conventional-changelog) from conventional commits; the spec's CHANGELOG criterion is satisfied by a conventional commit message at release time, NOT by hand-editing CHANGELOG.md.

No graph, verification, or context artifacts require edits: the change removes a log line and a documentation section without altering module contracts, public exports, dependencies, data flows, test strategy, critical scenarios, phase gates, or tracked log markers.
RTK is removed with no replacement tool and no detection/blocking logic, per the approved non-goals.

StaleStateWarnings.Warning: Implementation must read each target file fresh before editing; line numbers and excerpts in this plan are context anchors only and may be stale.

Do NOT hand-edit CHANGELOG.md; it is regenerated by release:bump from conventional commits. Satisfy the CHANGELOG criterion via a conventional commit message (e.g. fix(init): drop harmful RTK recommendation).

Do NOT edit .grace/graph or .grace/verification documents; this change has no module-contract, export, dependency, data-flow, test-strategy, or tracked-log-marker impact. If implementation evidence shows otherwise, stop and update the plan before expanding scope.
Do NOT add an RTK replacement, detection, or blocking logic; the approved spec intentionally limits this change to removal.

T-001.Title: Remove RTK recommendation from the interactive init outro.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: In src/commands/init.ts, edit the interactive p.outro() call so it passes only the success message, removing the "Highly recommended: Install RTK ..." lines, the rtk-ai/rtk install.sh curl, and the "rtk init -g --opencode" command.
No rtk-ai/rtk URL, "rtk", or RTK install command remains anywhere in src/commands/init.ts.
Add a CHANGE_SUMMARY LAST_CHANGE entry in src/commands/init.ts documenting that the harmful RTK recommendation was removed, following the existing entry format.
MODULE_CONTRACT and MODULE_MAP in src/commands/init.ts remain accurate; no export or contract change is made.

T-002.Title: Remove the Optional: RTK section from README.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: Remove the entire "## Optional: RTK" section from README.md, including the rtk-ai/rtk link and the claim that the interactive vvoc init flow recommends RTK.
Markdown section separators (---) remain balanced with no dangling or doubled separators left by the removal.
No rtk-ai/rtk link or RTK mention remains anywhere in README.md.
A repository-wide search for "rtk" returns no project-authored references, excluding transitive dependencies recorded in bun.lock.
typecheck, lint, format check, and bun test all pass.
GRACE lint accepts the updated change bundle (spec and plan).
If any expected verification command cannot be run, record the skipped command and reason before review or handoff.
